title: Kelun-Merck validate TROP2-Keytruda pairing in lung cancer with improved survival
snippet: Treatment with the TROP2 ADC sac-TMT led to a 70% objective response rate and progression-free survival was “significantly improved” as compared to placebo—the second positive readout for the asset this week.

title: Recordati fields $12.4B go-private offer
snippet: Going private could give Recordati strategic flexibility and a stable source of capital, according to CVC Capital Partners and Groupe Bruxelles Lambert, which are offering to take the Italian pharma private for a 13% premium.

title: Biogen, Denali pull plug on Parkinson’s program after mid-stage flop
snippet: Biogen and Denali’s Parkinson’s disease drug failed to significantly slow disease progression in a Phase 2b study, missing both primary and secondary endpoints.

title: Moderna’s once-rebuffed mRNA flu shot to face scrutiny from FDA adcomm
snippet: After getting slapped with a surprise refuse-to-file letter signed by former CBER Director Vinay Prasad, Moderna’s flu vaccine application will now go before the FDA’s Vaccine advisory committee.

title: Update: Reversal after 20 deaths allows new Japan patients to take Amgen's rare disease drug Tavneos
snippet: Kissei Pharmaceutical is reversing a recommendation related to Amgen-shared Tavneos that it made just a few days ago, now saying the rare disease drug can be given to new patients.

title: Roche licensing pact clears generic drugmakers to manufacture antiviral flu drug
snippet: By partnering with a UN-backed body, Roche has enabled companies to make the medicine for supply in 129 countries.

title: Lilly’s triple agonist shows bariatric surgery–like weight loss results
snippet: Lilly met analysts’ sky-high expectations with 28.3% weight loss over 80 weeks for the triple hormone receptor agonist retatrutide in a highly anticipated readout on Thursday.

title: Vaxart seeks shareholder support as battle over stock split brews
snippet: In September last year, a group of concerned stockholders raised alarm about Vaxart’s proposed reverse stock split, which the biotech was pushing for despite strong opposition from shareholders.

title: Lilly, BMS, Incyte strike deals to keep biopharma’s AI integration rolling
snippet: A trifecta of newly inked tech partnerships—from Eli Lilly, Bristol Myers Squibb and Incyte—exemplify the increasingly central role that AI is playing in drug development.

title: BioMarin bounces back as Voxzogo performs ‘bigger than expected’ in new indication
snippet: After being hit by safety issues and subpar results in another trial, BioMarin’s Phase 3 test of Voxzogo for a rare skeletal disorder called hypochondroplasia showed efficacy “solidly above” what the drug has shown for achondroplasia, which causes dwarfism.

title: Biopharma professionals increasingly accept underemployment
snippet: The number of biotech and pharma professionals taking jobs they’re overqualified for is now over 50%, based on a BioSpace LinkedIn poll. A recruiting firm executive discusses the trend, the reasons behind it and why it doesn’t have to derail careers.

title: Lilly and Engage Bio tie the knot with $202M acquisition
snippet: Eli Lilly joins hands with Engage Bio, acquiring the DNA delivery platform developer in hopes of bolstering its genetic medicines portfolio.

title: FDA sends warning letter after Chinese supplier breaks GLP-1 import restrictions
snippet: While the manufacturer is on the list of authorized GLP-1 importers, FDA inspectors found the company relabeled APIs from another site in a potential attempt to “circumvent safeguards.”

title: Pazdur declines to announce candidacy for vacated FDA commissioner role
snippet: FDA veteran Richard Pazdur outlined his priorities for the next FDA commissioner but stopped short of putting himself forward as a candidate to lead the agency at an RBC Capital Markets event on Tuesday.

title: Moderna’s stock rides hantavirus fears as spotlight returns to infectious disease platform
snippet: While the pathogen appears unlikely to trigger a pandemic, analysts see potential for Moderna to build goodwill amid a period of political pressure on vaccine manufacturers.

title: Beam one-ups Wave as both show promise of genetic editing for AATD
snippet: While both Beam Therapeutics and Wave Life Sciences touted notable biomarker benefits for their respective alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency assets, analysts said that Beam might have the efficacy advantage as Wave’s drug hits an efficacy “ceiling.”
